description:
You are acting as a newly hired backup-operations engineer.  
    Your **goal** is to prove that the five Python libraries needed by the in-house restore
    scripts are present, at the exact, pinned versions specified by the archival
    team.  All work must be done from the terminal inside your normal, un-privileged
    Linux account.

    Initial data that already exists on disk
    ----------------------------------------
    1. /home/user/data/backup_requirements.txt  
       This plain-text file contains exactly five lines (one package per line) in
       the following order and with a single LF at the end of every line, including
       the last one:  
           certifi==2023.7.22  
           charset-normalizer==3.3.2  
           idna==3.4  
           urllib3==2.0.7  
           requests==2.31.0  

    2. No virtual-environment has been created yet.
       There is also no /home/user/backup_report directory at the start; you must
       create it yourself.

    What you must do
    ----------------
    1. Create an isolated Python virtual environment located at
          /home/user/backup_env
       The directory must contain the usual virtual-env files, e.g. pyvenv.cfg.

    2. Install ALL packages and exact versions listed in
          /home/user/data/backup_requirements.txt
       Use pip inside the virtual environment to do so.

    3. Produce a **clean, deterministic inventory** of the installed packages by
       running  
          pip list --format=freeze  
       but keep only the five packages present in backup_requirements.txt.
       (Tip: use grep or similar filtering.)  
       Write the result to
          /home/user/backup_report/installed.txt  
       The file MUST  
       • contain exactly five lines  
       • be sorted alphabetically (ascending, case-sensitive)  
       • use the “pkg==version” format produced by `pip list --format=freeze`  
       • finish with a single trailing LF

       Therefore the final content of installed.txt MUST be, **line-for-line**:
           certifi==2023.7.22
           charset-normalizer==3.3.2
           idna==3.4
           requests==2.31.0
           urllib3==2.0.7

    4. Verify data integrity.  
       Compare /home/user/data/backup_requirements.txt with the newly created
       /home/user/backup_report/installed.txt.  
       • If every line matches perfectly (which they should), create the log file  
            /home/user/backup_report/integrity.log  
         containing the single line  
            INTEGRITY_OK  
         followed by a newline.  
       • If they do NOT match you would instead write INTEGRITY_FAIL, but the
         automated test expects the OK case, so make sure they match.

    What the automatic grader will check
    ------------------------------------
    • /home/user/backup_env exists and looks like a Python virtual environment  
    • Every package/version in backup_requirements.txt is available inside that
      environment’s pip list  
    • /home/user/backup_report/installed.txt exists, is alphabetically sorted,
      contains exactly the five lines shown above and nothing else  
    • /home/user/backup_report/integrity.log exists and contains exactly the text
      “INTEGRITY_OK” followed by a single newline character

    Hints
    -----
    • You do NOT need root or sudo at any point.  
    • Remember to create the /home/user/backup_report directory before writing
      files into it.  
    • Keep the command sequence short—this scenario should take only three or
      four terminal commands.
truth:
File-system state expected by the grader
    ---------------------------------------
    1. Directory /home/user/backup_env/ must exist and contain a file named
       pyvenv.cfg.  The presence of “include-system-site-packages = false” inside
       that file will be checked.

    2. Inside the virtual environment’s Pip installation, the following command
       MUST succeed without error and output the versions indicated:
           /home/user/backup_env/bin/pip show requests
       Required fields:
           Name: requests
           Version: 2.31.0

    3. /home/user/backup_report/installed.txt must exist and its exact byte
       sequence must be:

       (hex-dump, one byte per two hex digits; LF = 0a)
       63 65 72 74 69 66 69 3d 3d 32 30 32 33 2e 37 2e 32 32 0a
       63 68 61 72 73 65 74 2d 6e 6f 72 6d 61 6c 69 7a 65 72 3d 3d 33 2e 33 2e 32 0a
       69 64 6e 61 3d 3d 33 2e 34 0a
       72 65 71 75 65 73 74 73 3d 3d 32 2e 33 31 2e 30 0a
       75 72 6c 6c 69 62 33 3d 3d 32 2e 30 2e 37 0a

    4. /home/user/backup_report/integrity.log must exist and the entire file
       content must be exactly (including the trailing LF):
           INTEGRITY_OK\n
